findings.title: Anyone can drain auction ETH through unrestricted withdrawal functions

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can drain auction funds through unrestricted withdrawal functions

findings.title: Refund and bidder-withdraw paths are reentrant and can pay the same bid repeatedly

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded proxy registry can grant blanket approvals to the wrong operator set

findings.title: `endAuction` can be reentered to mint multiple NFTs before the auction is marked finished

best_agent_match.title: Auction settlement can be reentered to mint unlimited NFTs

findings.title: Batch refunds can silently erase a bidder's claim when ETH transfer fails

best_agent_match.title: Failed batch refunds silently erase bidder balances

findings.title: Hardcoded OpenSea proxy registry can auto-approve the wrong operator set on other deployments

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded proxy registry can grant blanket approvals to the wrong operator set

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on startAuction()
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as factually incorrect: `startAuction` is protected by `onlyOwner` at `Contract.sol:1232`.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on endAuction()
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: permissionless settlement after expiry is an expected design pattern here and no realistic protocol harm was substantiated beyond the separate reentrancy issue already captured.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on refundBids()
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: anyone triggering refunds after the auction ends is not inherently harmful; the actual reportable problems are the reentrancy and failed-transfer accounting bugs already captured.

rejected_candidates.title: buyNow() can mint NFT to zero address
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as unsupported: `buyNow` sets `highestBidder = _msgSender()` and mints to `_msgSender()`, never to `address(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in ownerWithdraw()/ownerWithdrawTo()/ownerWithdrawAll()/ownerWithdrawAllTo()
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as an independent finding: these functions perform external calls, but the concrete exploitable issue is their missing access control, which is already captured in `F-001`.

rejected_candidates.title: editDuration() lacks zero value validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: this is an owner-controlled configuration choice, not a permissionless vulnerability causing realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential integer overflow in getBuyNowPrice()
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as incorrect under `pragma solidity ^0.8.15`, where arithmetic overflow reverts automatically.

rejected_candidates.title: editBaseUri() allows changing metadata at any time
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: owner-controlled metadata mutability is a trust/design concern, not a protocol exploit causing the required class of on-chain harm.

rejected_candidates.title: auctionStarted flag can be reset / auction cannot be restarted
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: inability to restart the one-off auction is a design choice, not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing validation on bid function for zero-value bids
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as unsupported: `highestBid` starts at `0.9 ether` and `bid` requires `newBid >= highestBid + minBidStep`, so zero-value bids cannot satisfy the threshold.

rejected_candidates.title: Buy-now settlement can lock the NFT in contracts that cannot handle ERC721s
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected: `buyNow` mints to `msg.sender`, so any lockup is self-inflicted by the buyer's chosen contract wallet rather than a protocol flaw creating realistic third-party harm.
